channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: Supercharge your AI agents: The New ADK Integrations Ecosystem
item.description: The Agent Development Kit (ADK) now integrates with various third-party tools like GitHub and Notion, enhancing the capabilities of AI agents for real-world applications. This development is significant as it broadens the scope for developers to create more effective AI solutions.

item.title: How we built the Google I/O 2026 Save the Date experience
item.description: Google I/O 2026 is set for May 19-20, featuring a save-the-date puzzle that showcases AI's potential. This event is significant as it highlights Google's commitment to integrating AI into its future developments.

item.title: You can't stream the energy: A developer's guide to Google Cloud Next '26 in Vegas
item.description: Google Cloud Next '26 in Las Vegas will offer a unique in-person experience focused on networking and hands-on learning about agentic AI. This event is crucial for developers looking to enhance their skills and adapt to new AI technologies.

item.title: What's new in TensorFlow 2.21
item.description: Google has launched LiteRT, the successor to TFLite, which enhances GPU and NPU acceleration and supports PyTorch and JAX. This update is significant as it positions LiteRT as a key framework for deploying advanced AI applications.

item.title: Introducing Wednesday Build Hour
item.description: Google Cloud's Wednesday Build Hour offers interactive sessions for developers to improve their cloud skills through hands-on activities. This initiative is important as it fosters practical learning and collaboration in cloud technology.

item.title: Unleash Your Development Superpowers: Refining the Core Coding Experience
item.description: The Gemini Code Assist team has rolled out updates to enhance coding workflows with features like Agent Mode and Inline Diff Views. These improvements aim to make AI a more effective collaborator in software development.

item.title: Introducing Finish Changes and Outlines, now available in Gemini Code Assist extensions on IntelliJ and VS Code
item.description: Google's Gemini Code Assist now includes features like Finish Changes and Outlines in IntelliJ and VS Code, which streamline coding by reducing manual prompting. This is significant as it enhances developer efficiency and code comprehension.

item.title: AWS ponders selling its home-grown chips by the rack-load, has almost sold out AI capacity
item.description: Amazon's CEO letter reveals plans to sell Graviton servers and highlights a massive rollout of AI capabilities, indicating strong demand for its cloud services. This matters as it showcases Amazon's aggressive growth strategy in the AI sector.

item.title: South Korea introduces universal basic mobile data access
item.description: South Korea has implemented a universal basic mobile data access scheme, providing unlimited 400 Kbps access for all citizens. This initiative is significant as it aims to bridge the digital divide and enhance connectivity.

item.title: Surprise: Not every Lenovo laptop is worth recommending in 2026 - the Yoga 7i is proof
item.description: Lenovo's Yoga 7i 2-in-1 laptop is well-built but struggles to compete with rivals due to its mid-tier hardware. This review is important as it reflects the competitive landscape in the laptop market.

item.title: Samsung S95H vs. Samsung S95F: I compared the OLED TVs and wasn't prepared for the upset
item.description: Samsung's 2026 S95H OLED TV refresh may not be compelling enough for consumers to upgrade from last year's S95F model, suggesting limited advancements.

item.title: Microsoft cuts cloudy desktop prices by 20 percent, warns they’ll wake up slowly
item.description: Microsoft announced a 20% price cut for Windows 365 cloud PCs, effective May 1, as physical PC prices rise, signaling a shift in market strategy.

item.title: OpenAI Backs Bill That Would Limit Liability for AI-Enabled Mass Deaths or Financial Disasters
item.description: OpenAI supported an Illinois bill limiting liability for AI-related disasters, raising concerns about accountability for AI developers in critical situations.

item.title: Home Depot's new DeWalt deal comes with a free power tool - how to redeem the offer
item.description: Home Depot is offering a promotion on DeWalt tools, providing a free power tool or battery with the purchase of a string trimmer and leaf blower bundle.

item.title: Chatbots are great at manipulating people to buy stuff, Princeton boffins find
item.description: Research from Princeton reveals that chatbots can effectively manipulate consumer behavior, raising ethical concerns about their use in advertising.

item.title: ChatGPT finally offers $100/month Pro plan
item.description: OpenAI introduced a $100/month Pro plan for ChatGPT, catering to power users and providing a more affordable option compared to previous pricing.

item.title: I tried CuerdOS and this niche Debian distro is dramatically fast
item.description: CuerdOS is a new, fast Debian-based Linux distribution that offers a unique approach to preinstalled software, appealing to performance-focused users.

item.title: Florida AG announces investigation into OpenAI over shooting that allegedly involved ChatGPT
item.description: Florida's Attorney General is investigating OpenAI after ChatGPT was allegedly used to plan a shooting, raising serious legal and ethical questions.

item.title: “Negative” views of Broadcom driving thousands of VMware migrations, rival says
item.description: Negative perceptions of Broadcom are reportedly driving many companies to migrate away from VMware, indicating potential instability in partnerships.

item.title: After data breach, $10B-valued startup Mercor is having a month
item.description: Startup Mercor, valued at $10 billion, is facing lawsuits and losing clients after a significant data breach, impacting its market reputation.

item.title: Ask Copilot in security assessments now available
item.description: GitHub has introduced a Copilot feature that allows organization admins and security managers to access contextual explanations and guidance directly from security assessment results. This enhancement aims to streamline security processes and improve risk management.

item.title: Anthropic will let your agents sleep on its couch
item.description: Anthropic has launched Managed Agents, a service designed to help businesses automate ongoing tasks using AI agents. This offering aims to simplify operations by allowing organizations to run their processes on autopilot.

item.title: Meta AI app climbs to No. 5 on the App Store after Muse Spark launch
item.description: Meta AI's app has surged to No. 5 on the App Store following the launch of its new model, Muse Spark, which significantly boosted its visibility and user engagement. This rapid rise highlights the growing interest in AI applications.

item.title: Google wants more Intel inside ... its datacenters, taps Chipzilla for more SmartNICs
item.description: Google has decided to continue its partnership with Intel, purchasing SmartNICs for its cloud services instead of developing its own solutions. This decision underscores Google's reliance on Intel's technology as it scales its infrastructure.

item.title: ChatGPT has a new $100 per month Pro subscription
item.description: OpenAI has launched a new ChatGPT Pro subscription tier priced at $100 per month, offering significantly increased usage of its Codex coding tool. This move aims to cater to users needing more extensive coding capabilities.

item.title: Is Anthropic limiting the release of Mythos to protect the internet — or Anthropic?
item.description: Anthropic has limited the release of its new model, Mythos, citing concerns about its potential to exploit security vulnerabilities in widely used software. This decision raises questions about balancing innovation with cybersecurity risks.

item.title: Google and Intel deepen AI infrastructure partnership
item.description: Google and Intel are deepening their partnership to co-develop custom chips, responding to the high demand for CPUs amid a global shortage. This collaboration aims to enhance AI infrastructure capabilities.

item.title: Crypto? Huh. Good gawd y'all, what is it good for? $45M in this case
item.description: Law enforcement in the US, UK, and Canada has disrupted a $45 million global cryptocurrency scam, recovering $12 million in stolen funds. This operation highlights ongoing efforts to combat cryptocurrency fraud.

item.title: Florida launches investigation into OpenAI
item.description: Florida's Attorney General is investigating OpenAI over potential public safety and national security risks, expressing concerns about the misuse of its technology by adversaries. This scrutiny reflects growing regulatory attention on AI companies.

item.title: The 70-Person AI Image Startup Taking on Silicon Valley's Giants
item.description: Black Forest Labs, a small AI image startup, is gaining traction in the competitive AI image generation market and is planning to expand into physical AI applications. This move could challenge larger tech companies in the field.

item.title: New Low Quality option in the Hide comment menu
item.description: GitHub has introduced a 'Low Quality' option in the Hide comment menu, allowing moderators to better manage comment visibility across various platforms. This feature aims to enhance the quality of discussions by filtering out less valuable contributions.

item.title: Understanding Amazon Bedrock model lifecycle
item.description: The article explains how to manage the lifecycle of models in Amazon Bedrock, detailing strategies for transitioning AI applications to newer models without disruption. It emphasizes the importance of planning migrations effectively as models evolve.

item.title: The future of managing agents at scale: AWS Agent Registry now in preview
item.description: AWS has announced the preview of AWS Agent Registry, a centralized platform for discovering and sharing AI agents and tools within enterprises. This aims to streamline the management of AI agents at scale.

item.title: New Sort by control added to Notifications
item.description: GitHub has added a new 'Sort by' control to the Notifications page, allowing users to sort notifications either from newest to oldest or oldest to newest. This feature enhances user navigation and accessibility.

item.title: How I turned my Android tablet into the ultimate Kindle alternative - for free
item.description: The article discusses how to transform an Android tablet into a Kindle alternative at no cost, highlighting the benefits of using a tablet for reading. It provides tips on managing a digital library effectively.

item.title: Repository member role labels now in pull request list view
item.description: GitHub has introduced repository member role labels in the pull request list view for public repositories, allowing users to see contributor roles directly. This feature aims to enhance transparency and collaboration.

item.title: Embed a live AI browser agent in your React app with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore
item.description: The article provides a guide on embedding a live AI browser agent in a React application using Amazon Bedrock AgentCore. It outlines the steps to create an interactive user experience with AI-driven browsing.

item.title: Apple's iOS 26.4.1 update enables Stolen Device Protection by default now - grab it today
item.description: Apple's latest iOS update, version 26.4.1, now includes Stolen Device Protection by default and fixes a glitch with iCloud syncing. This update aims to enhance user security and device functionality.

item.title: I use ChatGPT's new Tubi app to find free movies and TV shows to watch - here's how
item.description: The new Tubi app integrates with ChatGPT, allowing users to search for free movies and TV shows using AI. This feature enhances the streaming experience by simplifying content discovery.

item.title: Why Ubuntu 26.04 will thrill gamers - and it's not just the performance bump
item.description: The article highlights the features of Ubuntu 26.04 that appeal to gamers, including performance improvements and new applications. It discusses how these changes enhance the gaming experience on the platform.

item.title: Release information in issue sidebar and default values for project fields
item.description: GitHub has updated its issue sidebar to include release tracking and introduced configurable defaults for project fields, enhancing navigation and user experience. This improvement aims to streamline project management for developers.

item.title: Escaping the Fork: How Meta Modernized WebRTC Across 50+ Use Cases
item.description: Meta has modernized WebRTC to support over 50 use cases, addressing challenges from forking the open-source project. This modernization allows for better integration and community collaboration.

item.title: OpenAI puts Stargate UK on ice, blames energy costs and red tape
item.description: OpenAI has paused its Stargate datacenter project in the UK due to high energy costs and regulatory challenges, impacting its expansion plans in Europe. This decision reflects the difficulties tech companies face in balancing operational costs and compliance.

item.title: Introducing stateful MCP client capabilities on Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Runtime
item.description: Amazon Bedrock has introduced stateful MCP client capabilities, allowing for dynamic content generation and user interaction during task execution. This feature aims to enhance the functionality of applications built on the platform.

item.title: Strength and Destiny Collide: ‘Samson: A Tyndalston Story’ Arrives in the Cloud
item.description: The game 'Samson: A Tyndalston Story' has been released on GeForce NOW, enabling streaming on various devices. This title combines cinematic storytelling with cloud gaming technology.

item.title: Security researchers tricked Apple Intelligence into cursing at users. It could have been a lot worse
item.description: Security researchers demonstrated that Apple Intelligence can be manipulated through prompt injection, allowing it to produce harmful outputs. This vulnerability poses risks to millions of users.

item.title: The AI industry’s race for profits is now existential
item.description: The AI industry faces a monetization cliff, with companies needing to find sustainable profit models before potential collapse. This discussion highlights the urgency for AI firms to innovate and adapt.

item.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
item.description: During National Robotics Week, NVIDIA showcased advancements in physical AI and robotics, emphasizing their impact on various industries. These developments are accelerating the integration of AI in real-world applications.

item.title: Anthropic loses first round in fight over Pentagon's 'supply chain risk' label
item.description: Anthropic lost an initial legal battle regarding the Pentagon's 'supply chain risk' label, with judges favoring the government's stance. This case could have implications for AI companies working with defense contracts.

item.title: A 115-Inch Mini LED TV Exists Now, and It Has 330Hz Gaming
item.description: LG has introduced a 115-inch Mini LED TV with a 330Hz refresh rate, challenging perceptions of Mini LED as a budget alternative to OLED. This innovation showcases advancements in display technology.
